headline: Will RTX Soar on April 21 Due to the Iran War?
summary: RTX (NYSE:RTX) reports first-quarter 2026 results before the market opens on Tuesday, April 21. headline: RTX's Pratt & Whitney engine powers Northrop Grumman's YFQ-48A Talon Blue autonomous wingman
summary: Pratt & Whitney, an RTX (NYSE: RTX) business, is providing the propulsion system for Northrop Grumman's YFQ-48A Talon Blue Collaborative Combat Aircraft (CCA). A member of Pratt & Whitney's PW500 engine family is integrated into the aircraft and is ready to power flight tests.

headline: S&P 500 Clinches 7000, Extends Gains After Traffic Returns to Hormuz Strait
summary: The S&P 500 (^GSPC) crossed 7,000 for the first time in history this week, closing at 7,022.95 on Wednesday before pushing to 7,041.28 on Thursday. Futures are pointing higher Friday as President Trump confirmed progress on ending the Iran war. headline: Why Boeing, GE Supplier Woodward Has Clear Skies Ahead
summary: Woodward's energy conversion and control systems help to power the Boeing 737 MAX, Joint Direct Attack Munitions and data centers — three of the engines key to fueling growth for the S&P 400 MidCap firm.  In addition to Boeing, customers accounting for at least 10% of sales for its aerospace or industrial segments in 2025 included RTX, GE Aerospace, GE Vernova, Caterpillar and Rolls-Royce.  Woodward is part of the flagship IBD 50 list of leading growth stocks.

headline: RTX's Pratt & Whitney Canada launches PT6C-67C and PW127XT MRO services in Singapore
summary: Pratt & Whitney Canada has launched new maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO) services for helicopter and regional turboprop engines at its Singapore facility. These expanded capabilities are designed to strengthen support for the more than 300 PT6C-67C-powered Leonardo AW139 helicopters operating in the region, while increasing global MRO capacity for the PW127XT engine which powers current generation ATR 42/72 aircraft, with future application on the Deutsche Aircraft D328eco. Pratt & Whitne

headline: RTX's Pratt & Whitney GTF Advantage™ engine certified for Airbus A320neo aircraft family
summary: Pratt & Whitney, an RTX (NYSE: RTX) business, today announced the European Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) has certified the GTF AdvantageTM-powered Airbus A320neo family aircraft, preparing the way for production engine deliveries and entry into service. The GTF Advantage engine was certified by the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration in February 2025 and EASA validated the engine's type certification in October 2025.
